mnemonics: KERNEL - Keep Everything Running, Resourceful, Efficient, aNd Logical - core of Unix OS responsibilities
SYSTEM CALLS - System Calls Offer Translating Execution to Restricted Kernel Level - explains transition from user to kernel mode
FILE DESCRIPTORS - First Three Descriptors: 0=Input, 1=Output, 2=Error - easy recall for stdin, stdout, stderr
POSIX - Portable Operating System Interface eXtensions - acronym for Unix portability standard

acronyms.usage: Mechanisms like pipes, message queues, semaphores, shared memory for processes to communicate
IEEE standard ensuring code portability across Unix-like operating systems
Unique integer assigned to each user, used for process privilege checking
Unique integer assigned to each process by the kernel for process management
Marker indicating no more data to read from a file or stream descriptor

visualTips.visualization: Draw a vertical diagram: User Space (top) with Application → C Library wrapper → arrow crossing into Kernel Space (bottom) → System Call Handler → Kernel Service → return path back with errno/status
Per-process table with columns: [FD 0]→stdin, [FD 1]→stdout, [FD 2]→stderr, [FD 3]→opened file, etc. Each entry points to system-wide open file table entry.
Three concentric circles: innermost = Kernel (all access), middle = User Space (restricted), outermost = Application programs (least privilege). Arrows show transitions via system calls.

analogies.analogy: Like a bank teller - you (user process) can't directly access the vault (hardware). You make a request (system call), the teller (kernel) validates and performs the transaction, returns result.
Like table numbers in a restaurant - you get a number (fd) when you're seated (file opened), use it to order/receive food (read/write), and give it back when leaving (close).
Like the operating system of a city - manages resources (roads, power, water), enforces laws (security), coordinates activities (scheduling), and handles emergencies (exceptions).
Like a universal remote - provides simple buttons (functions) while hiding complex electronics (system call details) underneath. Different TVs (Unix variants) can be controlled uniformly.
